name: Savannah Cobos
ai_summary: Savannah Cobos is a Utah-based childcare professional with experience working with children from infancy to pre-teen, including those with autism and as an RBT. She is passionate, reliable, and skilled at conflict resolution, making her a strong candidate for UpKid teaching roles.

transcripts.q3:
Hi, my name is Savannah and I have pretty good experience working with kids. I have worked at to daycares. And at both daycares, I worked with kids from, like, six months, old up until like 7, and 8. I'd say, so, I have a lot of age range. They're working with pretty small babies and then the older kids, I also have experience working with children with autism. I did, I was an R&B.
 ET from like February to August. So a couple of months and I worked with a five-year-old and then a three-year-old. So that was pretty fun and I have good age range there and I also taught English and India and my kids were about one up into 12 so I have experience working with pretty much all ages household of four younger siblings, so I kind of grew up just around kids so that's pretty fun.
 And I enjoy working with kids because it actually feels like I'm doing something and making a difference and I just think it's a fun work environment and you get to talk to a bunch of little kids and hang out with them, which I think is just so fun. And yeah, I just love working with kids.
transcripts.q4: Yeah, so I live in Eagle Mountain Utah and I'm pretty much willing to travel wherever in Utah that's like close enough.
transcripts.q5: I have pretty much open availability and looking for a part-time or full-time such as kind of whatever is available. I am happy with
transcripts.q6: Away. I have dealt with conflict is if another child has hit a child. I would take the initial child who hid and I would bring them away from that child, and then I would talk to them about how we can use safe hands in the classroom and how we shouldn't hit our friends and we can give them high fives or bones if they want to and just talk to them about using safe hands and why that's important.
transcripts.q7:
I would make a great up kid teacher because I love working with kids. It's just something I'm very passionate about and I feel like I work very hard. I'm on time to every job I've ever had and I don't call out. I also think that I could just add some fun into the classroom and just be someone who is responsible and who shows up and I just initially,
 love working with kids. So I think I would be a great addition.
